ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for 3-in-1 instant milk tea; closest defensible parent is 'instant tea beverage' (FOODON_03301099). The 3-in-1 format combining tea powder, non-dairy creamer, and reduced sugar is not individually coded in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP (Tea-based beverages) is the closest defensible parent category. No specific FoodEx2 code exists for instant 3-in-1 premix tea with non-dairy creamer and reduced sugar.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for instant 3-in-1 milk tea premix; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

allergens: milk (non-dairy creamer may contain milk derivatives)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one sachet (approximately 20 g powder) dissolved in 180 ml hot water, yielding roughly 200 ml of prepared beverage.

nutrient_source.title: 3-in-1 Milk Tea (Less Sugar) — Singapore HPB Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for '3 in 1 milk tea (less sugar)', cr_id D09020009, confidence 0.95. All per-100g macros and micronutrients are sourced from this entry.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for 3-in-1 instant milk tea (less sugar). GI estimated at 55 based on the closest analogous entry for sweetened instant tea beverages with milk/creamer from the University of Sydney GI Database. Per the validator classification boundary applied in this project, GI 55 is categorised as 'low' (threshold: low ≤55, medium 56–69, high ≥70). Glycemic load calculated as GI × carbohydrate_g per serving / 100 = 55 × (9.33 × 200/100) / 100 = 55 × 18.66 / 100 ≈ 10.3 per 200 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar driving insulin-mediated sodium retention; non-dairy creamer saturated fat content
hypertension.guidance: Although sodium is low at 35 mg/100 g, the added sugar (4.65 g/100 g) in this sweetened instant mix promotes insulin-mediated sodium retention, and the non-dairy creamer likely contains palm-based saturated fat; patients with hypertension should limit consumption and prefer plain unsweetened tea.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar (4.65 g/100 g) classifying this as a sugar-sweetened beverage; GI of 55 with moderate glycaemic load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Despite the 'less sugar' label, this instant premix contains 4.65 g added sugar per 100 g and a GI of approximately 55, yielding a glycaemic load of approximately 10 per standard serving; individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid this beverage and substitute plain unsweetened black tea.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Non-dairy creamer contributing saturated fat (~2.3 g/100 g) and potential trans fat from partially hydrogenated vegetable oil
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The non-dairy creamer in this 3-in-1 premix likely contains palm kernel oil or partially hydrogenated vegetable oil, contributing saturated fat (~2.3 g/100 g) and possible trace trans fat; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake and choose plain tea without creamer.

llm_training.natural_language_description: 3-in-1 Milk Tea (Less Sugar) is an ultra-processed instant beverage popular in Singapore and across Southeast Asia. It consists of a single sachet containing black tea powder, non-dairy creamer, and a reduced quantity of sugar, designed to be dissolved in hot water to produce a ready-to-drink milk tea. The 'less sugar' designation indicates a lower sugar content compared to the standard 3-in-1 formulation, though added sugar remains present at 4.65 g per 100 g of prepared beverage. The taste is mildly sweet and creamy with a light black tea flavour, and the texture is smooth and liquid. Per 100 g of prepared beverage, this drink provides 64 kcal, 0.45 g protein, 2.65 g fat (predominantly saturated from non-dairy creamer), and 9.33 g carbohydrate including 4.65 g sugar. Dietary fibre is low at 0.97 g/100 g and sodium is relatively low at 35 mg/100 g. Calcium is reported as 0 mg/100 g in the HPB database, which is notable given the creamer component and may reflect the prepared-beverage dilution basis or the specific formulation. Clinically, despite the reduced sugar label, this beverage is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) under ADA and WHO guidelines and is not recommended for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The non-dairy creamer fraction likely contributes palm-based saturated fat and potentially trace trans fat from partially hydrogenated vegetable oil, making it pro-inflammatory and unfavourable for cardiovascular and rheumatoid arthritis management. Plain unsweetened black tea is the preferred alternative for health-conscious consumers.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D09020009) with a recorded confidence of 0.95. The source is an approved primary regional source for Singapore foods. The match is exact. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for sweetened instant tea, which is the closest approved GI source entry available.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this instant 3-in-1 milk tea (less sugar) are not individually reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry D09020009 as of 2026-04-16. 2. calcium_mg is reported as 0.0 mg/100 g in the HPB source (confirmed zero per HPB Foodo D09020009); this is notable given the non-dairy creamer component and may reflect the prepared-beverage dilution basis or the specific formulation. 3. saturated_fat_g estimated at 2.3 g/100 g based on the fat fraction (2.65 g/100 g total fat) being predominantly palm-based saturated fat from non-dairy creamer; this is an approximation as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. 4. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for sweetened instant tea beverages; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in approved sources. gi_category is set to 'low' per the project validator boundary (low ≤55, medium 56–69, high ≥70). 5. Dietary tags 'low_sodium' and 'low_calorie' from the input are retained as contextually accurate (sodium 35 mg/100 g; energy 64 kcal/100 g) but do not override the clinical concern ratings for diabetes and cardiovascular disease. 6. Halal status assumed based on typical formulation of Singapore-market instant tea premixes; label verification recommended. 7. ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type set to 'closest_parent' (valid enumerated value) as no exact ontology code exists for this specific 3-in-1 instant milk tea premix format.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Documented all -1 sentinel values in the 'notes' field to satisfy g3_numeric_hygiene. Listed all fields set to -1 and stated the reason for data unavailability (not reported in HPB source).
Iteration 2: Attempted to correct ontology match types to 'approximate'; validator rejected as 'approximate' is not in the allowed enumeration ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'].

Iteration 3: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent', which is a valid enumerated value per the scoring model. This resolves the g5_ontology_honesty hard gate failure. Also corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' per the validator boundary (GI 55 falls at the upper boundary of 'low': low ≤55, medium 56–69, high ≥70), resolving the q4_glycemic_completeness failure.
